title: Understanding the Position Function
text: The position function is given by $$x(t)=p t^3+q t^2$$. To find the force, we need to first find the acceleration. How can we find acceleration from the position function?

title: Finding Velocity
text: To find the velocity, differentiate the position function $$x(t)=p t^3+q t^2$$ with respect to time $$t$$. What is the expression for velocity v(t)?

title: Velocity Expression
text: Differentiate $$x(t)=p t^3+q t^2$$ with respect to $$t$$ to find v(t).

title: Finding Acceleration
text: Now, differentiate the velocity function $$v(t)=3p t^2+2q t$$ with respect to time $$t$$ to find the acceleration a(t).

title: Acceleration Expression
text: Differentiate $$v(t)=3p t^2+2q t$$ with respect to $$t$$ to find a(t).

title: Using Newton's Second Law
text: According to Newton's second law, the net force F on an object is given by $$F=m a$$, where $$m$$ is the mass and a is the acceleration. Use this to find the net force on the object.

title: Net Force Expression
text: Using $$F=m a$$ and the expression for a(t), find the net force F(t).
